mechanism_narrative: A declining FEDFUNDS rate lowers discount rates and eases financial conditions, which should lift equity valuations and produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is confirmed when rate cuts are sustained rather than a one-off move.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in a majority of periods where FEDFUNDS declines for at least 2 consecutive months, or if the rolling 3-year hit rate falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above a recent baseline signals weakening household income and demand, which should pressure corporate earnings expectations and drive negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the effect disappears after controlling for the prior 3-month trend in UNRATE.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS accelerates while HOUST remains weak, consumer spending is outpacing housing demand and the market reads the mix as resilient near-term activity without overheating pressure, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of rising RSAFS and flat-to-soft HOUST does not outperform either proxy alone, or if 5-day forward returns are not positive in at least 55% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in INDPRO signals weakening industrial output and earnings momentum, which should weigh on cyclicals and push S&P 500 returns lower over the next quarter. The effect is strongest when the production slowdown is broad rather than noisy.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward SPX returns are not negative after 3-month declines in INDPRO, or if the signal has a rolling 3-year Rank IC above 0.03 in the opposite direction.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in HOUST above trend indicates stronger housing demand and upstream activity, which should improve growth-sensitive earnings expectations and generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises by at least 8% versus its 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if post-trigger 20-day SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of cases, or if the effect vanishes when HOUST growth is below its 12-month median.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation stays elevated while FEDFUNDS is still below the inflation pace, real rates remain restrictive only in name and the Fed is likely behind the curve. Equity multiples should compress and S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation remains above FEDFUNDS for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: Reject if the inflation-above-policy-rate condition does not produce negative 60-day SPX returns in a majority of episodes, or if the direction reverses after accounting for recent CPI trend.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in PAYEMS and fall in UNRATE signals broad labor-market strengthening, which supports consumer income growth and earnings revisions, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-up and UNRATE-down condition does not outperform either labor proxy alone, or if triggered cases fail to show positive 20-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, steady GDPC1 growth with rising RSAFS indicates still-healthy final demand, which should support risk appetite and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The mechanism fails if GDP growth decelerates while sales weaken.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with stable-to-rising GDPC1 and RSAFS do not deliver positive 60-day SPX returns in at least 55% of cases, or if the signal loses all predictive power when GDP growth slows below its prior 2-quarter average.
